output.final_deliverable:
Chain of pages (in visit order):
1) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
2) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents
3) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Mathematics
4) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mathematics

One verified adjacency from the provided visit data (from the merged results):
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents -> https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
  Evidence: the Portal:Contents visit’s links_sample includes "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page" (see the Portal:Contents visit result at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents).

Notes about missing evidence (explicit in the merged results):
- The merged results do not show a links_sample entry demonstrating that https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page links to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents, so Main_Page -> Portal:Contents is not verifiable from the provided samples.
- The merged results do not show a links_sample entry demonstrating that https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents links to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Mathematics, so Portal:Contents -> Portal:Mathematics is not verifiable from the provided samples.
- The merged results do not show a links_sample entry demonstrating that https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Mathematics links to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mathematics, so Portal:Mathematics -> Mathematics is not verifiable from the provided samples.

(Additional link evidence present in the merged results: Portal:Mathematics’ links_sample does include https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Current_events, but that page is not part of the above chain.)
output.action_summary: I started at the Main Page and visited the sequence Main Page -> Portal:Contents -> Portal:Mathematics -> Mathematics (these four URLs are the visited chain). From the merged visit data, the only directly verifiable adjacency shown in the provided links_sample is Portal:Contents -> Main_Page (Portal:Contents’ links_sample contains the Main Page URL). The merged results do not include explicit links_sample entries that verify the other consecutive adjacencies in the chain (Main Page -> Portal:Contents, Portal:Contents -> Portal:Mathematics, or Portal:Mathematics -> Mathematics). URLs cited are from the merged visit results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Mathematics, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mathematics.

result.title: Assemble chain and produce verified adjacency evidence

details.intent: Collect outputs from the four visit nodes, assemble the ordered chain of URLs, and extract the adjacency evidence (a link line demonstrating that Portal:Mathematics links to the Mathematics article).
target_facts: Ordered array of visited URLs (length >= 4) in the order visited.
One verified adjacency object: {source_url, target_url, evidence_snippet} where evidence_snippet is the exact link line returned by the Portal:Mathematics visit showing the target_url.
If any expected link is missing at runtime, produce a fallback adjacency using another link discovered on the previous page (and document why).
details.extraction_plan: After child visits complete, read their extracted link lists and content. Build the chain using the chosen links. For the verified adjacency, take the exact link line from the Portal:Mathematics visit's _links_inline that matches the Mathematics article URL and include it verbatim as evidence.
details.rationale: This think node is required to consolidate child outputs and produce the final deliverable (ordered chain + verified adjacency). It ensures the chain satisfies the rule that each next URL was found on the previous page and provides verifiable evidence.
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

1ac681d7-a9ca-478c-9a91-6f38138b1ca4.title: Assemble chain and produce verified adjacency evidence

details.intent: Collect outputs from the four visit nodes, assemble the ordered chain of URLs, and extract the adjacency evidence (a link line demonstrating that Portal:Mathematics links to the Mathematics article).
target_facts: Ordered array of visited URLs (length >= 4) in the order visited.
One verified adjacency object: {source_url, target_url, evidence_snippet} where evidence_snippet is the exact link line returned by the Portal:Mathematics visit showing the target_url.
If any expected link is missing at runtime, produce a fallback adjacency using another link discovered on the previous page (and document why).
details.extraction_plan: After child visits complete, read their extracted link lists and content. Build the chain using the chosen links. For the verified adjacency, take the exact link line from the Portal:Mathematics visit's _links_inline that matches the Mathematics article URL and include it verbatim as evidence.
details.rationale: This think node is required to consolidate child outputs and produce the final deliverable (ordered chain + verified adjacency). It ensures the chain satisfies the rule that each next URL was found on the previous page and provides verifiable evidence.
details.parent_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
details.original_goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Merge: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
